name: Erebus, the Darkness
tips: Erebus costs 20 mana minus your Dark Matter pool. At 15 Dark Matter he's a 5-mana 80/80, and at 20+ he's free — the most efficient body in the game.
Grow the pool with Shady Trade Deal and Dark Matter Stash before dropping Erebus. The entire Dark Matter archetype exists to make this card affordable.
Erebus is himself a Dark Mage — once on board he counts toward every future gain (Shady Trade Deal, Stash, Illicit Shadow Stones) and receives Dark Matter Stash's buff.
Playing Erebus doesn't spend the pool — the cost reduction only READS it. Your Barrage / Matter of Time / Battery keep their full scaling after he lands.
Don't wait for 0-cost — playing Erebus at 8-10 mana is still a dominant play. Holding too long risks losing your Dark Mages and pool momentum.
rulings: cost_reduction=dark_matter subtracts YOUR Dark Matter pool (a player resource, visible to both players) from the printed 20 mana at play time. Minimum cost is clamped at 0 — with 20+ Dark Matter, Erebus is free.
The cost reduction reads the pool without consuming it — the pool stays intact after Erebus is played.
Erebus is a [[Dark Mage]] (element Dark, tribe Mage), so he counts toward per-Dark-Mage gains and receives ally-Dark-Mage buffs after he's on board.
Dying resets all his buffs (he respawns only via Revive-type effects, none of which currently target him). Your Dark Matter pool is unaffected by his death.
